title: BUbreakingHeadlines
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbreakingHeadlines, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: Massachusetts governor pushes back after RFK Jr. calls out popular coffee chains
items.description: HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is challenging Starbucks and Dunkin' over the safety of high sugar drinks for teenagers as part of his Make Americ...

items.title: Britney Spears arrested in Southern California, Ventura County Sheriff's Office records show
items.description: According to Ventura County inmate records, Spears was arrested around 9:28 p.m. Wednesday and released around 6:07 a.m. on Thursday.

items.title: Noem's testimony on contracts 'false,' Democratic senator claims
items.description: DHS Secretary Kristi Noem appeared on Wednesday in front of a House committee.

items.title: Companies are entitled to Trump tariff refunds, trade court rules
items.description: A federal court in New York ruled Wednesday that businesses that paid emergency tariffs invalidated by the Supreme Court are eligible for refunds.

items.title: How Kurdish forces could help the U.S. fight the Iran war
items.description: Kurdish forces surrounding Iran may jump into the fight against the Islamic Republic. CBS News' Courtney Kealy reports.

items.title: WATCH:  'Brady Bunch' house designated as historical-cultural monument
items.description: The L.A. City Council designated the iconic home as a historical-cultural monument, protecting the beloved landmark from demolition.

items.title: Iran's drone and missile strikes continue, hit new ground as war spreads

items.title: Iran live updates: US torpedo attack on Iran warship 'an atrocity' minister says
items.description: IDF airstrikes targeted "dozens" of sites connected to internal security forces.

items.title: What to know about the investigation into Catholic priests in Rhode Island and sexual abuse charges
Last 2 names of 6 US soldiers who died in Kuwait attack identified by the Pentagon
items.description: The last two names of the six U.S. soldiers killed in a Kuwait attack have been released by the Pentagon, and they are from California and Iowa.
